title: A whole-canvas SVG frontend cap can be signed GREEN while rendering offscreen — fit-to-view framing is a component-side viewBox the ADR-0070 geometry/look split leaves unowned and unglueable
description: Building library-overview (inc 5 of the library-tech-tree-overlay arc), the signed --real leaf laid every corpus node out in origin-centred user-units via stressSeeds (x mean 0, spanning ~thousands of px for 393 nodes) inside an <svg> with NO viewBox and no width/height framing. Every machine contract (element-count, tiers, band, layout totality/determinism, glow, select, no-fetch) passed and the verdict signed GREEN, because none of them frames the field. But a raw SVG with no viewBox maps user-units 1:1 from its top-left origin, so most of an origin-centred cloud renders offscreen. Framing it fit-to-view needs a viewBox (or a bbox-centred <g transform>) — both are DOM attributes INSIDE the signed component, not CSS-settable, so the orchestrator's after-PASS glue/CSS canNOT fix it (unlike inc-3's real-data crash, which WAS glue-fixable at the mount via the libraryAssets memo).
statement: When a frontend cap renders a WHOLE-canvas SVG (a constellation/map/graph spanning arbitrary user-coordinates), fit-to-view framing (a viewBox or a bbox-centred transform) is deterministic geometry — but ADR-0070 routes 'appearance' to the operator-attested look leg, so a leaf can omit the viewBox and still sign GREEN on element-count/layout-totality contracts while the field renders mostly offscreen. And because a viewBox is a DOM attribute on the signed <svg>, not a CSS property, the orchestrator's after-PASS look-leg glue/CSS cannot retrofit the framing without editing byte-locked signed source. The framing thus falls in the gap between the machine geometry (which didn't require it) and the glue (which can't add it) — the opposite of inc-3's mount-boundary-fixable real-data crash. Contrast: the inc-3 focus-subgraph used @dagrejs/dagre, which returns bounded positions, so it never hit this; a whole-corpus stressSeeds field does.
evidence: apps/studio/src/components/LibraryOverview.tsx (signed @ 0730e31, byte-locked) renders `<svg className="library-overview-field" ...>` with NO viewBox; nodes are `<g transform={translate(pos.x,pos.y)}>` where pos comes from constellationLayout→stressSeeds (LAYOUT_RADIUS 40, unit 220 → L~300, spread ~ L*sqrt(393) ≈ 5900px, origin-centred). Live staging walk (studio-json-5175, real corpus 393 nodes = 179 artifacts + 214 ADRs) rendered all 393 <g> nodes with zero console errors, but only the central slice sits in the ~1280x720 viewport. The only mitigation available in glue was a CSS `.library-overview-field { transform: translate(50%,50%); overflow: visible }` (index.css) to seat the origin at the pane centre — it centres the dense core but does NOT fit-to-view (the outer cloud still overflows), because CSS cannot set a viewBox. The signed 8/8 contracts (lov-*) include layout totality/determinism + FAR element-count but none asserts a bounded/fit viewBox, so the sign was honest yet the look renders unframed.
impact: The operator-attested look leg handed to the owner renders the constellation unframed (central-slice only), so the owner attestation for inc-5's overview look is degraded and a fit-to-view/pan-zoom framing is a required follow-up increment rather than part of the signed unit — and it is NOT orchestrator-glueable (needs a signed-source edit / a new cap contract). Hits any future whole-canvas SVG frontend cap (maps, constellations, node graphs over arbitrary coordinates): unless the spec makes a deterministic fit-to-view viewBox/bbox a GEOMETRY contract (e.g. 'the rendered viewBox bounds every node', bbox-totality — machine-testable), the leaf can sign GREEN on a field that renders offscreen and the orchestrator cannot retrofit framing in glue. Candidate routes: a principle/spec-authoring rule that a whole-canvas SVG cap's GEOMETRY must include a bounded fit-to-view contract; or a story-author/planner checklist item for SVG caps.
